rulings.Case Title: MARY NICHOLS VS. MARK STREHLOW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 06, 2017 line 1. PLAINTIFF MARY NICHOLS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES GRANTED. Defendant's ANTI-SLAPP motion was devoid of merit. Plaintiff is entitled to $15,819 in fees. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MARY NICHOLS VS. MARK STREHLOW ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 06, 2017 line 1. DEFENDANT MARK STREHLOW'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. As an initial matter, the Court has not ruled on the merits of the original demurrer. In the March 29, 2016 Order (approved as to form by all parties) the Court indicated that the hearing on the Demurrer will be held at a later date. The hearing did not take place on that date due to the pending appeal. Defendant filed his demurrer after remittitur. Opposition and Reply on this round of briefing is different from the papers submitted for the original demurrer. Additionally, based on Tobener declaration there has been a significant change in circumstances, specifically that Defendant amended his Estoppel Certificate and does not claim disability anymore. Based on the new briefing the Court rules as follows: Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is overruled. According to the Rent Board rules (See Ex. A to Norman Declaration) the Rent Board will nor provide a declaration regarding disability. Sending this case to the Rent Board would be a futile act. However, the Plaintiff should not re-allege this cause of action in the amended complaint if there is no ripe controversy; it appears based on Tobener declaration that Defendant does not claim disability any longer. Demurrer to causes of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to identify "in some manner" a "specific" buyer. Ramona Manor v. Care Enterprises (1986) 177 Cal.3d 1120. Demurrer to these causes of action is otherwise overruled. Demurrer to the cause of action for elder abuse is overruled. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged. Bounds v. Sup. Ct. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 468; Mahan v. Charles W. Chan Insurance Agency (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 841. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: AMIT DOW ET AL VS. 1) DEAN CLEMENTS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 06, 2017 line 5. DEFENDANTS DEAN CLEMENTS, STACIE CLEMENTS, PAUL MCKENNA, AND JAMES GALLAGHER'S MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED. Plaintiffs' claims subject to this motion are barred by the statute of limitations and Plaintiffs failed to allege facts showing equitable tolling. Plaintiffs' own Rent Board Petition was not filed until September 19, 2016. Plaintiffs fail to cite any authority for the proposition that participation in the Rent Board proceedings regarding Defendant's Petition for rent increase was one of multiple legal remedies available to Plaintiffs and pursued in good faith in order to obtain relief to the same claims that are alleged in this action. Plaintiffs did not oppose Defendants' motion to strike "The 'Procedural History' section" Notice of Motion 1:28 and "Paragraph 9 of the Common Allegations." Id. at 2:1. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA CHAN VS. GIGI CARLISLE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Admit And Compel Discovery Responses Against Fred Mendoza And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 06, 2017 line 6. PLAINTIFF BRENDA CHAN's Motion To Admit And Compel Discovery Responses Against Fred Mendoza And For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Request for Production and Form Interrogatories within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $810. = (501/AJR)
